title: 3 FilterBoxBuilder: Variant Models and Fabrication Types

steps.title: Overview: Variant Models
steps.description: The settings tour named the inputs. This tour names the output structures that travel between modules: layout results, cut panels, printable parts, 3MF mesh objects, Corsi models, donut filter models, and static reference metadata.

steps.title: Layout Result
steps.description: The layout result carries both user-facing summary data and machine-facing geometry. Most other modules receive this object rather than reading raw form values.

steps.title: Cut Features
steps.description: Cut features are typed geometry operations: circles, rectangles, paths, labels, and optional operation metadata. The laser and preview paths share this representation.

steps.title: Cut Panel
steps.description: A cut panel combines physical dimensions, a sheet placement, an outline, features, and optional assembly metadata. That assembly metadata later tells the preview how to position the part.

steps.title: Printable Kit
steps.description: The printable kit groups print parts, the chosen bed preset, print sheets, and summary counts. It is the common output for generated and specialized 3D print designs.

steps.title: 3MF Mesh Object
steps.description: 3MF export is driven by mesh objects and plates. Mesh data stays independent from DOM and Three.js scene objects, so export can run in the browser without depending on the preview scene.

steps.title: Corsi Model
steps.description: The Corsi model stores topology-specific facts such as which faces hold filters, which face holds fans, and how many fan cassettes the top panel needs.

steps.title: Donut Filter Model
steps.description: The donut model is a different print design vocabulary. It has an adapter, cap, cylindrical filter, and fan guard rather than rectangular panels and filter rails.

steps.title: Static Print Reference
steps.description: Static references point at curated STL assets, license metadata, preview assembly assets, and print plate assets. They enter the same UI as parametric generators, but their source geometry is external.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: Which structure lets the app handle generated kits and curated STL references through one print-design selector?
steps.explanation: `PrintDesignModel` starts at line 169 in `src/domain/purifier/airPurifier.ts` and has branches for generated, Corsi, donut, and static reference designs.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: The app uses explicit structures for each physical artifact: panels, features, printable parts, meshes, plates, and static references. The next tour traces how raw settings become a populated layout result.
